body_txt: Most people have trouble reaching things because of height . Some characters, though, can actually be pretty tall for most things yet still can't reach anything. In some cases, they can't reach things that people shorter than them can reach just fine. Why is this? It's because their arms are so small that they don't even reach their legs without bending over if even then. This can lead to quite a few scenarios where they will reach for something only to fail because of how short their arms are, whether that be grabbing objects, opening doors, or even just making gestures with their arms. Many of these struggles happen with human-made objects that were never designed for short-limbed creatures to handle . Big theropod dinosaurs are particularly well-known for having proportionally puny arms. Science Tangent Why exactly these animals had such small arms has been a subject of debate among paleontologists since their discovery in the 19th century. One leading hypothesis is that natural selection favored big heads and long legs, and the arms became vestigial as a trade-off — some Speculative Biology works with Alternate-History Dinosaur Survival have the theropods becoming Armless Bipeds tens of millions of years after what would have been The Day the Dinosaurs Died . This most commonly pops up with the iconic — and famously short-armed — Tyrannosaurus rex , with the even shorter-armed Carnotaurus having become a popular choice as well since its discovery in 1984 (this is also true of their respective tyrannosaurid and abelisaurid relatives, though those two are the most popular choices for this trope). Conversely, there were other theropods that actually had relatively long arms and thus aren't usually portrayed this way, among them the spinosaurids and raptors , the latter of which are often stereotyped as being almost as intelligent as humans and thus able to do all sorts of things with their hands. Classic Carnosaurs in early to mid-20th-century movies and TV shows were also frequently depicted with inaccurately longer arms and more dexterous hands (which had a lot to do with many of them being portrayed by People in Rubber Suits who had to put their arms somewhere ). Sometimes overlaps with Cephalothorax and T. Rexpy . Contrast Cumbersome Claws (when big and/or sharp claws are a hindrance) and Creepily Long Arms . See also Feather Fingers and Humanlike Hand Anatomy for when animals and animalistic characters have dexterous hands even if they wouldn't in real life.
